data.title: Finland's UPM Q1 adjusted EBITDA beats, boosted by energy segment
data.description:
Overview

  • Finland material solutions firm's Q1 revenue slightly beat analyst expectations

  • Q1 adjusted EBIT and adjusted EBITDA both beat analyst expectations

  • Company highlighted strong decarbonization solutions and record energy segment results

Outlook

  • UPM expects H1 2026 comparable EBIT of €325-525 mln

  • Company expects H1 2026 to benefit from higher sales prices, delivery volumes, and lower fixed costs

  • Performance in H1 2026 expected to be held back by weak paper markets and ramp-up costs at UPM Leuna

Result Drivers

  • ENERGY SEGMENT - Record Q1 results in UPM Energy supported by cold winter and record electricity consumption in Finland

  • BIOFUELS PERFORMANCE - UPM Biofuels continued to improve and posted strong results

  • ADVANCED MATERIALS - Adhesive Materials and Specialty Materials delivered steady, resilient performance with increased deliveries versus previous quarter

data.title: Spain's Mapfre profit rises despite lower premiums on currency hit
data.description:
By Marta Serafinko and Mireia Merino

Spanish insurer Mapfre reported on Wednesday a 12.7% rise in its first-quarter net profit, supported by its non-life insurance business - its core property and casualty segment - and the absence of large catastrophe losses, as well as higher investment income from portfolio yields and realised gains.

Mapfre's net result of 310.9 million euros ($363.7 million) compares with 275.9 million euros a year earlier. Premiums slightly fell to 8.39 billion euros from 8.58 billion euros in the same period last year, dampened by currency depreciation, particularly of the U.S. dollar.

Weather-related events, including storms in Iberia and floods in southern Turkey during the quarter, affected the results, though the company did not quantify the total cost.

The group's non-life combined ratio, a key measure of an insurer's profitability, improved to 93.2%. A ratio below 100% means the company earns more in premiums than it pays out in claims.

Hyperinflation adjustments in Turkey dented the quarterly result by 7.7 million euros, compared with a 6 million euro hit a year earlier.

Iberia, the main contributor to group profit, had a net result of 137.8 million euros, up nearly 14%, despite Portugal's 0.4 million euro loss due to storms. The Latin America region delivered a net income of 113.7 million euros which translated into a 3.9% fall from the same period last year, mainly due to weaker results in the region despite Brazil's contribution.

Chairman Antonio Huertas said in the statement that the results confirm Mapfre is on track to meet the targets of its strategic plan despite a complex geopolitical environment.

The insurer said it would pay a final dividend of 11 cents gross per share on May 28.

($1 = 0.8546 euros)

data.title: Iberdrola ups adjusted profit growth guidance to 8%
data.description:
Europe's largest utility Iberdrola on Wednesday upped its guidance for this year's adjusted profit growth to more than 8% from around 6% as it posted a first-quarter adjusted net profit of 1.865 billion euros ($2.18 billion), 11.4% higher than a year ago.

Iberdrola said that discounting the foreign exchange impact of 99 million euros from the depreciation of the dollar, the pound and the Brazilian real, adjusted net profit would have grown 17% in the quarter.

Reported net income that discounts capital gains from asset rotation fell 15% to 1.71 billion euros, largely in line with the median forecast by analysts polled by LSEG of 1.72 billion euros.

The company said its earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortisation (EBITDA) totalled 4.07 billion euros, down from 4.5 billion a year earlier and slightly above the median forecast of 4 billion, adding that in forex-adjusted terms, EBITDA would have grown 6%.

($1 = 0.8547 euros)

data.title: Taiwan's ASE Technology Q1 revenue rises on ATM segment growth
data.description:
Overview

  • Taiwan semiconductor assembly and EMS firm's unaudited Q1 revenue rose 17% yr/yr

  • Diluted EPS for Q1 grew to NT$3.08 from NT$1.64 a year earlier

  • Gross margin improved to 20.1% from 19.5% in the prior quarter

Outlook

  • Company did not provide specific guidance or outlook for the current quarter or full year in its press release

Result Drivers

  • ATM SEGMENT GROWTH - Revenue from assembly, testing and materials operations rose 29.7% yr/yr and 2.5% sequentially, supporting overall group performance

  • EMS SEGMENT WEAKNESS - Revenue from electronic manufacturing services fell 0.7% yr/yr and 10.3% sequentially, weighing on consolidated revenue growth

  • MARGIN IMPROVEMENT - Gross margin at the group level increased to 20.1% from 19.5% in the prior quarter, reflecting lower cost of revenues

data.title: Silicon Motion Technology Q1 Profit Rises On Higher Revenue; Sees Q2 Sales Growth
data.description:
TAIPEI (dpa-AFX) - Silicon Motion Technology Corp. (SIMO) reported strong first-quarter performance, with significant growth in revenue and profitability compared with the same period last year.

Operating income rose to $52.20 million from $9.77 million in the prior-year quarter.

Net income increased to $66.79 million or $1.97 per ADS from $19.46 million or $0.58 per ADS a year earlier. Excluding one-time items, adjusted earnings were $53.85 million or $1.58 per ADS.

Revenue grew to $342.11 million from $166.49 million last year.

For the second quarter, the company expects revenue growth of 98% to 107% to $393 million to $411 million.

data.title: Spanish stocks - Factors to watch on April 29
data.description:
The following Spanish stocks may be affected by newspaper reports and other factors on Wednesday. Reuters has not verified the newspaper reports, and cannot vouch for their accuracy:

Naturgy

Spanish power company Naturgy reported on Wednesday a first-quarter net profit of 530 million euros ($620 million), about 5% higher than a year ago, beating the median forecast by analysts polled by LSEG of 525 million euros, on higher power generation and energy prices.

IBERDROLA

Europe's largest utility Iberdrola posted on Wednesday a first-quarter net profit of 1.71 billion euros ($2.00 billion), largely in line with the median forecast by analysts polled by LSEG of 1.72 billion euros, after its electricity output rose 1.6%.

The company said on Tuesday its Brazilian subsidiary Neoenergia strengthened its strategic partnership with GIC, which will join seven transmission projects for 2.42 billion reais (around 412 million euros).

SANTANDER

Spain's Santander on Wednesday said its underlying net profit rose 12.5% year-on-year in the first quarter supported by higher revenues and fees which offset higher provisions.

AMADEUS

Amadeus announced intention to buy Idemia Public Security for 1.2 billion euros plus earn-out up to 150 million euros.

REDEIA

The company reported Q1 net profit of 140.3 million euros.

AENA

The company reported Q1 net profit of 329.4 million euros.

MAPFRE

Spanish insurer Mapfre said on Wednesday its net profit in the first quarter rose 12.7% driven primarily by its non-life insurance business amid the absence of large catastrophe losses, as well as higher financial income from portfolio yields and realised gains.

PUIG

Spanish beauty company Puig reported slower sales growth for the first quarter on Tuesday, reflecting more moderate demand particularly in fragrances, which account for the bulk of its revenue, though it kept its full-year outlook unchanged.

SOLARIA

The company said on Wednesday the total amount raised in private placement of shares amounted to 299.9 million euros.

RENTA 4

Renta 4 posted on Tuesday Q1 net profit of 12.6 million euros.
